claims.claim: Adaptive Boundary Colorization Gate produced positive/supportive evidence in the recorded OMX run.
claims.allowed_wording: The current project artifacts support this finding in the tested setting.
claims.forbidden_wording: This proves the method works universally.

claims.claim: The recommended next action is: Finalize this branch as a supported deterministic adaptive gate; if more work is queued, optimize gate latency and test on a larger held-out retrieval sweep rather than branching again.
claims.allowed_wording: The project decision recommends this follow-up.
claims.forbidden_wording: This follow-up is guaranteed to succeed.

limitations: This draft is automatically generated for human review and must be checked before public release.
Claims are limited to the project artifacts listed in the evidence bundle.
External replication and broader hardware/model coverage are TODO unless explicitly present in the artifacts.

Project stop/decision rationale: The gate preserved tight/late-evidence colorization gains and eliminated the known 480-token technical-doc regression by routing to vanilla when the answer contract was already visible.
forbidden_global_claims: Do not claim peer review or public validation.
Do not claim results on datasets, hardware, or models not present in artifacts.
Do not hide negative or inconclusive findings.
